label: Value of Travel Time Savings (DOT guidance)
description: US DOT values travel time as a percentage of the prevailing wage rate, varying by trip purpose. Unlike UK TAG (fixed values per hour), the US approach scales automatically with wage levels.

local_personal.label: Local personal travel (commuting, errands, leisure)
local_personal.note: 50% of hourly wage. Based on stated preference research showing personal time valued at roughly half the work rate.

intercity_personal.label: Intercity personal travel
intercity_personal.note: 70% of hourly wage. Higher than local because intercity trips are less discretionary and travelers have higher willingness to pay.

all_business.label: Business/employers' business travel
all_business.note: 100% of hourly wage plus fringe benefits. Full cost to employer.

walking_waiting_transfer.label: Walking, waiting, and transfer time
walking_waiting_transfer.note: 100% of the relevant category rate (personal or business). Perceived as more onerous than in-vehicle time.

reference_wage.source_detail: Bureau of Labor Statistics, Occupational Employment and Wage Statistics (OEWS), May 2023

computed_vtts.description: Current VTTS based on median hourly wage
computed_vtts.note: Business uses mean wage (employers pay average, not median). Personal uses median. These are national averages; use local wage data for project-specific analysis.

car_per_mile.note: AAA average for sedans (fuel, maintenance, tires). Does not include depreciation or insurance.

source.publication: Revised Departmental Guidance on Valuation of Travel Time in Economic Analysis; BCA Guidance for Discretionary Grant Programs 2025

caveats: Percentage-of-wage approach means VTTS varies by region. Use local BLS wage data for project-specific analysis.
DOT also requires sensitivity analysis at 3% and 7% discount rates (legacy A-4) alongside the revised 2% rate.
Freight VTTS is separate and depends on commodity value, reliability, and logistics costs.
